prompt: Photoreal sports photograph from the Intergalactic Football Worldcup (IFW). SCENE: dramatic low frontal angle, in the Nimbora Arena — solid tiered stands ringing a floating cloud-shelf platform, high-key sunrise sky and the banded gas-giant only as a backdrop beyond the rim, a proper flat football pitch of even turf with white markings; a striker attacks a dropping ball. Every Nimbora Skydrifters player looks EXACTLY like the feathered-alien reference; every Marentide Tides player looks EXACTLY like the amphibian-alien reference. Replicate each reference creature precisely — identical head, body, limbs, proportions and markings — do NOT restyle, elongate or humanise. Kit: Skydrifters pale-rose/lavender; Tides teal/seafoam (shirt, shorts, socks, boots). POSES — DERIVED FROM ANATOMY, NOT human football poses: - Skydrifters striker attacks the ball like a raptor: leaping off both digitigrade bird-legs with a downward wing-beat for lift, body pitched back, tail-feathers fanned for balance, striking the dropping ball with the clawed sole of one raised foot, the other leg folded beneath, wings fully spread, eyes on the ball. - Tides defender holds a low coiled amphibian crouch: hind legs deeply bent and splayed, body low and forward, long tail extended behind as counterbalance, webbed hands spread, crest raised, head tilted up, loaded to spring. BALL: an ordinary matte black-and-white football, sharp and visible, NOT glowing. CROWD: the stands hold a mixed alien audience as distinct, separated figures in tiered rows, facing the pitch — not merging into the sky or stands. Realism: 70mm dramatic low front, shallow depth of field, players and ball sharp, NO motion blur, soft warm sunrise key, authentic texture and contact shadows. Photoreal — NOT CGI, no plastic skin, no human poses, no restyled anatomy, no glowing ball, crowd not merging with the arena, no text errors; invented crests only, no real-world logos. 16:9 aspect ratio.
